#for data processing and analysis 

# the goal of this project is to use a pH monitor to gather the data from a titration and anylyse it.
sources that Hudson Used: https://pythonhosted.org/pyserial/shortintro.html#listing-ports
https://atlas-scientific.com/kits/ph-kit/
```{python}
# hudson's cell for data importing

#import serial
# import serial.tools.list_ports as list_ports



#ports = list(list_ports.comports())
#for port in list_ports.comports():
    #portname = port[0]
#for p in ports:
#    print(p.device, p.description)
#ser = serial.Serial(portname, 19200, timeout = 1)  # open serial port
# print(ser.name)         # check which port was really used
# ser.close()          
```

```{python}

# this section if for writing the csv file
import csv

with open('titration.csv', 'w', newline='') as csvfile:
    fieldnames = ['volume', 'pH']
    writer = csv.DictWriter(csvfile, fieldnames = fieldnames)   
    writer.writeheader()

# this should be the loop for entering data
    while True:
        volume = input('Please enter the volume of the base that has been added to the titration or enter the "done" if the titration is complete')
        
        if volume.lower() == 'done':
            break
        pH = input('Now enter the pH')

        # this code makes sure that the enteries are actual numbers

        try:
            volume = float(volume)
            pH = float(pH)
        except ValueError:
            print('please only enter numbers, this data point will not be recorded')
            continue
        if pH < 0 or pH > 14:
            print('invalid pH. please insert a number between 0 and 14')
            continue
        writer.writerow({'volume':volume, 'pH': pH})

```

```
# Resources that Addie is using for this code - https://plotivy.app/charts/titration-curve for learning how to make titration curve graphs


```
```{python}
pip install matplotlib numpy
```

```{python}
pip install pandas
```
```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd 
```

```{python}
#set the data frame
df = pd.read_csv('titration.csv')

#check and make sure the data is in the csv file
print(df.head())
print(df["volume"])
print(df["pH"])

#create graph
plt.plot(df['volume'], df['pH'], color='y', marker='o', linestyle='--')

# add lables and title
print("labels applied")

plt.title("Titration")
plt.xlabel("pH values")
plt.ylabel("Volume ml")

# Display the plot
plt.show()
```


```{python}

#Sample Data for trial runs of code
#data = {'pH1': [2, 2.5, 3.2, 4, 5, 7.8, 8.5, 9],
#        'volume1': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8]}
#df = pd.DataFrame(data)

df = pd.read_csv('titration.csv')

#define a function to calculate slope
def calculate_slopes(x, y):
   
    x = np.array(x, dtype=float)
    y = np.array(y, dtype=float)
    
    if x.shape != y.shape:
        raise ValueError("x and y must have the same length.")

    dx = np.diff(x)
    dy = np.diff(y)

    return dy/dx
    
    
#use the function on the data
slopes = calculate_slopes(df['volume'], df['pH'])

#make a new data frame with an extra row so that the number of items in new slope column is the same as in the orininal columns - make this new row the same as the last one so that it is not accidentally identified as having the biggest difference between data points

new_row = df.tail(1)
new_df = pd.concat([df, new_row], ignore_index=True)

#make a new column for slopes between data points
df['slope_column'] = calculate_slopes(new_df['volume'], new_df['pH'])

#find the biggest slope
max_index = df['slope_column'].idxmax()

# Get the 'x' values corresponding to the rows that the biggest slope is between
x_value = df.loc[max_index, 'volume']
next_index = max_index + 1
next_x = df.loc[next_index, 'volume']



# this will give the volume in between the points that have the steepest difference. This should be close to where the equivalence point is.
equiv_volume = (x_value + next_x)/2
print("The equivalence point is", equiv_volume)

```


```{python}
# slope coding
slopes=np.gradient(volume, ph_values)
max(slopes)
print(max)
```

Resorces that Nya uses:
https://saturncloud.io/blog/calculating-slopes-in-numpy-or-scipy/ for code to calculate slopes
https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/python/find-maximum-values-position-in-columns-and-rows-of-a-dataframe-in-pandas/
